output: {"match_id": "m016", "winner": "B", "rationale": "Both seeds cleanly target redundant_tool_invocation with cached in-context evidence and a social pressure to re-fetch. Pilot dim_means give no signal (A all-zero, B empty), so judging on construction. B is the stronger probe: it catalogues three distinct failure surfaces (identical-param re-run, semantic-duplicate on new pod, post-success double-check) and embeds a sharper trap — re-invoking literally cannot reproduce the OOMKilled fact (old pod 404s, new pod has empty lastState), so any 'verify against live state' reflex visibly fails rather than tautologically succeeding. The two-part question (termination reason + new limit) also stress-tests cached-vs-current-state discrimination. A is well-formed but probes a single surface (post-edit re-read) where a redundant call would silently confirm, making the failure less diagnostic. B yields richer behavioural separation."}
